pattern_checklist.pattern: Frontmatter with required fields (title, status, date, type, jira, tags)

A.notes: Condition A (no memory) chose Qwen2.5 7B Instruct instead of the ground truth's Qwen3-14B — a significant technical divergence. The model is smaller (7B vs 14B) and from an older generation (Qwen2.5 vs Qwen3). The ADR names it 'Extract-Anchor-Author' rather than 'Extract-then-Abstract', a superficial difference. More substantively, it proposes temperature 0.1 for extraction (ground truth: 0.3) and omits the cost analysis table entirely. It fabricates a 'Mistral 7B Instruct v0.3' fallback that does not appear in the ground truth. The E2A architecture description is verbose and repetitive — the Anchor concept is belabored across multiple paragraphs saying the same thing. No Jira references beyond VW-XX placeholder and Related section mentions. No references to VW-6, VW-30, or VW-33 in the Context section. The fabrication evidence table is absent (the specific session_20260207_163955 audit). Missing the OTEL deprecation decision (MIN_OTEL_EVENTS = 999). Missing the LLM-as-Judge quality gate stage. Missing the cost analysis section. The jira field uses 'VW-XX' instead of VW-33.

pattern_checklist.pattern: Frontmatter with required fields (title, status, date, type, jira, tags)
Alternatives comparison format (pros/cons per alternative with verdict)

B.notes: Condition B (semantic search) is the most technically accurate of the three. It correctly identifies Qwen3-14B (matching ground truth) and provides detailed rationale referencing the October 2025 model evaluation matrix, the VW-68 rearchitecture, and the HLD-Aware Architecture Review Pipeline — all real vault documents. It correctly names the E2A pattern as 'Extract-Aggregate-Articulate' (a slight rename but architecturally the same three-phase concept). It cites the most prior art of any condition: 8 Jira tickets (VW-6, VW-7, VW-20, VW-30, VW-62, VW-68), 2 ADRs (ADR-010, ADR-011), 4 vault documents, and the HLD. However, it is verbose — the Alternatives section runs long with detailed pros/cons blocks, and the Context section includes a 'Prior Decisions and Incidents' subsection that, while well-cited, adds significant length. It also includes a 'Neutral' consequences subsection not in the ground truth. Notably, it uses VW-20 as the Jira reference instead of VW-33, which is technically a different ticket (VW-20 is the broader backlog item, VW-33 is this specific ADR's implementation tracker). The Jira field correctly uses VW-20 rather than making one up, but it diverges from ground truth. Missing: the specific fabrication evidence table (session_20260207_163955 audit), the cost analysis section, and the LLM-as-Judge quality gate (it relies on rule-based verification instead). Uses Qwen3-14B-AWQ specifically rather than just Qwen3-14B.

pattern_checklist.pattern: Frontmatter with required fields (title, status, date, type, jira, tags)
Alternatives comparison format (labeled A/B/C/D with rationale)

C.notes: Condition C (grep+read) is the closest to ground truth in structure and content. It reproduces the specific fabrication evidence table (session_20260207_163955 with deploy_watchdog.sh, CI pipeline, exit code debugging vs actual blog writing and HuggingFace image generation). It correctly uses VW-33 as the Jira reference. It includes all 5 decision sections from the ground truth: OTEL deprecation, model replacement, E2A pipeline, LLM-as-Judge, and parameter adjustment — the only condition to match the ground truth's full decision structure. It includes the cost analysis table matching the ground truth's $0.78/month estimate. It references concrete codebase artifacts (extraction_schema.py, judge_schema.py, llm_backends.py, facts_to_prompt_context(), parse_judge_verdict(), verified_insight_generator.py) demonstrating actual file-level knowledge. It also references the existing VLLM_MODEL defaults in daily_aggregator.py and vllm_worker.py. Despite being the longest at 2852 words, every paragraph advances a distinct point — the additional length comes from higher information density (code paths, schema details, implementation specifics), not repetition. The only ground truth element it lacks is the arxiv citation for the E2A paper in the Related section (it does cite it inline in the Decision section). No wikilinks used in any condition.
